title: Remove and inspect crankshaft/crank pulse plate

crossRefs.label: Transmission End Crankshaft Oil Seal Installation

blocks.text: Remove and inspect crankshaft/crank pulse plate
Loosen the bearing cap bolts several times in numerical order and remove the bearing cap bridge and bearing cap.

items: The bearing cap bolts should not be loosened all at once, but several times diagonally from outside to inside.
Before disassembling, check the mounting position of each component and mount them in the same position when installing.

items.text: The bearing cap bolts should not be loosened all at once, but several times diagonally from outside to inside.
The bearing cap bolts should not be loosened all at once, but several times diagonally from outside to inside.
Before disassembling, check the mounting position of each component and mount them in the same position when installing.
Before disassembling, check the mounting position of each component and mount them in the same position when installing.

blocks.text: Remove the connecting rod cap (A) and connecting rod bearing (B).
Remove the crankshaft (A) so as not to damage the main journal and pin journal. Remove the transmission end oil seal (C) and crank pulse plate. Remove the thrust washer (D).

items: The connecting rod caps must be aligned with the bottom dead center of each cylinder before removal.
Do not damage the crank pulse plate (B).

items.text: The connecting rod caps must be aligned with the bottom dead center of each cylinder before removal.
The connecting rod caps must be aligned with the bottom dead center of each cylinder before removal.
Do not damage the crank pulse plate (B).
Do not damage the crank pulse plate (B).

blocks.text: Measure roundness at the center of the journal. circularity Std. 0.005 mm or less Limit value: 1 0.010mm

items: Roundness shall be measured at points A1 and B1 of the outside diameter of the main journal and pin journal of the crankshaft, and the difference between A1 and B1 shall be determined.
If the limit value is exceeded, the crankshaft shall be replaced.

items.text: Roundness shall be measured at points A1 and B1 of the outside diameter of the main journal and pin journal of the crankshaft, and the difference between A1 and B1 shall be determined.
Roundness shall be measured at points A1 and B1 of the outside diameter of the main journal and pin journal of the crankshaft, and the difference between A1 and B1 shall be determined.
If the limit value is exceeded, the crankshaft shall be replaced.
If the limit value is exceeded, the crankshaft shall be replaced.

items: Taper (cylindricity) shall be measured at points A and B of the outside diameter of the main journal and pin journal of the crankshaft, and the difference between A and B shall be determined.
If the limit value is exceeded, the crankshaft shall be replaced.

blocks.text: Using a surface plate and V-block, measure the runout of the crankshaft. Crankshaft runout Std. 0.03 mm or less Limit value: 1 0.04mm

items: The journal sections at both ends shall be supported by V-blocks.
Inspect the crankshaft runout with a dial gauge.
Inspect while rotating the crankshaft.
If the limit value is exceeded, the crankshaft shall be replaced.

items.text: The journal sections at both ends shall be supported by V-blocks.
The journal sections at both ends shall be supported by V-blocks.
Inspect the crankshaft runout with a dial gauge.
Inspect the crankshaft runout with a dial gauge.
Inspect while rotating the crankshaft.
Inspect while rotating the crankshaft.
If the limit value is exceeded, the crankshaft shall be replaced.
If the limit value is exceeded, the crankshaft shall be replaced.

blocks.text: Apply engine oil to the threads and seating surfaces of the mounting bolts, install the crank pulse plate (A) onto the crankshaft (B), and mount the crankshaft onto the cylinder block.

items: The crank pulse plate shall be installed with the model mark (C) facing outward and the tab (D) on the pulse plate aligned with the groove (E) on the crankshaft.
Do not damage the crank pulse plate.

items.text: The crank pulse plate shall be installed with the model mark (C) facing outward and the tab (D) on the pulse plate aligned with the groove (E) on the crankshaft.
The crank pulse plate shall be installed with the model mark (C) facing outward and the tab (D) on the pulse plate aligned with the groove (E) on the crankshaft.
Do not damage the crank pulse plate.
Do not damage the crank pulse plate.

blocks.text: Install thrust washer (A) with engine oil on No.2 journal.

items: Thrust washers should be mounted so that their flat faces face each other.

blocks.text: Measure the outside diameter of the connecting rod bolt at two locations and determine the difference in outside diameters. Conrod Bolt O.D. Difference Std. 0－0.05mm

items: A micrometer should be used for the measurement.
Measurements shall be taken on all connecting rod bolts.
If the difference in outside diameter is greater than the standard value, the connecting rod bolt should be replaced.

items.text: A micrometer should be used for the measurement.
A micrometer should be used for the measurement.
Measurements shall be taken on all connecting rod bolts.
Measurements shall be taken on all connecting rod bolts.
If the difference in outside diameter is greater than the standard value, the connecting rod bolt should be replaced.
If the difference in outside diameter is greater than the standard value, the connecting rod bolt should be replaced.

blocks.text: Install connecting rod cap (A) and connecting rod bearing (B).
Align the connecting rod caps with the bottom dead center of each cylinder and then tighten temporarily.

items: When installing the connecting rod cap, be sure to align the mating marks (A) so as to avoid misorientation.
Do not drop the thrust washer when rotating the crankshaft.

items.text: When installing the connecting rod cap, be sure to align the mating marks (A) so as to avoid misorientation.
When installing the connecting rod cap, be sure to align the mating marks (A) so as to avoid misorientation.
Do not drop the thrust washer when rotating the crankshaft.
Do not drop the thrust washer when rotating the crankshaft.

items: If the specified tightening angle is exceeded when tightening the connecting rod bolts, remove the bolts and retighten them after performing the connecting rod bolt inspection procedure again.

blocks.text: Remove old fluid packings, oil, and dirt from the cylinder block and No. 4 bearing cap (A). Assemble No. 1 bearing cap (B) and No. 4 bearing cap to bearing cap bridge (C).

items: When installing the bearing caps, they must be installed in the same position and orientation as in the assembled condition.

blocks.text: Apply Honda Genuine Liquid Packing 1216E to the cylinder block contact surface of the No. 4 bearing cap.

items: Apply liquid packing with a wire diameter of about 2.5 mm along the dashed line (A).
If the liquid packing is left on for more than 4 minutes after application, do not install it as it is. Remove the packing once and reapply.
Oil should be injected at least 30 minutes after installation.
Do not touch the liquid packing-applied surface.
Wipe off any fluid packing that protrudes from the crankshaft pulley and drive plate side.
The engine should be started at least 3 hours after installation.

blocks.text: Install the bearing, bearing cap, and bearing cap bridge, and tighten the bearing cap bolts in numerical order to 33 N-m (3.4 kgf-m).
9. Transmission End Crankshaft Oil Seal Installation

refs.label: Transmission End Crankshaft Oil Seal Installation

items: The oil seal must be installed with the part number facing outward.
When a new bearing is assembled, it should be broken in at idle for 10-20 minutes to prevent separation or seizure of the bearing if the rotation is increased too rapidly.
